title: Bindings, Scope, Closures, and Lifetime · Behavior Workshop
summary: Build configurable counters and validators without leaking mutable state across community-service widgets. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a closure-backed state and validation library while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose const, let, or legacy var from reassignment, scope, and initialization behavior.
Resolve names through lexical environments and identify shadowing without guessing from call order.
Predict declaration instantiation, function hoisting, and temporal dead-zone failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Create two independent status counters seeded at 3 and 6. Calls must preserve closure isolation, explicit inputs and returns, and a trace showing why a shadowed binding does not update outer state.
